Not all election challenges are frivolous and cynical. Sometimes there are illegal/ineligible ballots in a close election, and if a candidate raises a legal challenge, a court must make a decision: certify the original results? adjust the results somehow, possibly reversing the election? order a new election? The concern of course is that the presence of the bad ballots might have swung the outcome, but how likely is this? Let's model it.

a.  derive hypergeom pmf
b.  what's the event
c.  compute it for these numbers.
d.  generalize
e.  plot
f.  add table to plot
g.  vague discussion

```{r}
#| echo: false
# https://www.nyulawreview.org/wp-content/uploads/2018/08/NYULawReview-83-1-Hickey.pdf
# https://doi.org/10.1198/jasa.2009.ap08640
```
